facade_colour: Mixed: ground floor various cladding; upper floors original red brick and painted surfaces

hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: Each property has value as an early-twentieth-century House-form building that reflects architectural styles of the period. Through its construction date and typology, each property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. Constructed as a semi-detached pair, the properties retain many original building features including their mirrored facades and shared roofline, stepped brick gable parapets, dormer windows, second-storey bay windows, and segmentally arched openings on the first storey. 92 Bellevue Avenue additionally retains its brick cladding. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street. Each property supports and maintains the residential character of the streetscape and is functionally, visually, and historically linked to the District.

context.street_character: One-way | Residential street | Tree-lined | Heritage character

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: mixed: ground floor has various cladding including corrugated metal and wood; upper floors retain original buff/cream brick with red brick accents
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Ground floor heavily altered with various non-original cladding materials. Upper floors retain original brickwork. Semi-detached house-form with gable is still legible despite ground-floor alterations.
photo_observations.window_details: Ground floor: 2 windows (altered frames). Second floor: 2 windows in bay projection retaining original proportions. Third floor (gable): 1 small window.
photo_observations.door_details: Ground floor entrance with wooden deck; multiple material patches
photo_observations.bay_window_details: Second-storey bay window retained; mirrored pair with 92
photo_observations.porch_details: Open porch with wooden deck at ground level, new construction visible.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Original elements obscured by alterations; satellite dish on facade
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Significant ground-floor alterations: corrugated metal cladding, new wooden deck, mixed materials. Upper floors retain original brick. Bay window form preserved. Overall house-form still legible.
photo_observations.notes: matches_hcd corrected from false to true. Despite heavy ground-floor alterations, the semi-detached house-form with gable roof and bay window is still clearly legible in the upper storeys. The building form matches the HCD typology even though materials are degraded.

notes: Semi-detached with 92. Unlike 92, has lost significant heritage character through ground-floor alterations.

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 3 storeys and is capped by a gable, consistent with its house-form, semi-detached typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 2, 1 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Character-defining facade elements include bay window, original windows, cornice.
facade_detail.heritage_summary: Constructed as a semi-detached pair, the properties retain many original building features including their mirrored facades and shared roofline, stepped brick gable parapets, dormer windows, second-storey bay windows, and segmentally arched openings on the first storey. 92 Bellevue Avenue additionally retains its brick cladding.

facade_description: Semi-detached three-storey house-form in mixed: ground floor various cladding; upper floors original red brick and painted surfaces with a gable roof. Features include a double-height projecting bay and simple bargeboards. Significant ground-floor alterations: corrugated metal cladding, new wooden deck, mixed materials. Upper floors retain original brick. Bay window form preserved. Overall house-form still legible. Constructed 1904-1913.
heritage_summary: Constructed as a semi-detached pair, the properties retain many original building features including their mirrored facades and shared roofline, stepped brick gable parapets, dormer windows, second-storey bay windows, and segmentally arched openings on the first storey. 92 Bellevue Avenue additionally retains its brick cladding.
